arabic: ٱقۡتَرَبَ لِلنَّاسِ حِسَابُهُمۡ وَهُمۡ فِي غَفۡلَةٖ مُّعۡرِضُونَ
transliteration: Iqtaraba lilnnasi hisabuhum wahum fee ghaflatin muAAridoona
translation: For the human beings, their time of reckoning is near, and yet they pass their time in utter recklessness.
notes: The time of reckoning is the Judgment Day in the Hereafter. And man is wont to think that Day is far off. Already over 1400 years have elapsed since this Verse was revealed, and Allah alone knows how many more centuries hereafter will this event occur. Man is thus deluded into doubts whether that event will ever occur. And he relapses to his worldliness. He starts thinking that this world is the be-all and end-all of all things. So why not grab whatever you get in this life, by fair means or foul, and make merry? Who has seen the morrow, so why worry about it? This attitude is the root cause of all trouble and has the potential to convert this very world into Hell. Allah Ta’ala therefore warns mankind against this attitude and delusion by telling them that they should not be deceived by mere appearances. No person is secure from death, which may come the very next moment. And when that person rises from the dead, it will appear to him/her as if he/she had been dead for hardly some hours! So, in that sense, the time of reckoning for any person should be near. It is to that person’s benefit then that he should not while away his/her precious time in this world in its frivolous and temporary pleasures.

arabic: مَا يَأۡتِيهِم مِّن ذِكۡرٖ مِّن رَّبِّهِم مُّحۡدَثٍ إِلَّا ٱسۡتَمَعُوهُ وَهُمۡ يَلۡعَبُونَ
transliteration: Ma yateehim min thikrin min rabbihim muhdathin illa istamaAAoohu wahum yalAAaboona
translation: No aspect of divinity comes to them from their Lord in the form of a fresh anecdote, but they listen to it in jest
notes: Muslims did just that during the first few centuries after the revelation of the Qur’aan and climbed to pinnacles of glory in all constructive fields of life. But thereafter they lapsed into negligence towards the divine Book and fell to depths of ignominy as of now.

arabic: لَاهِيَةٗ قُلُوبُهُمۡۗ وَأَسَرُّواْ ٱلنَّجۡوَى ٱلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُواْ هَلۡ هَٰذَآ إِلَّا بَشَرٞ مِّثۡلُكُمۡۖ أَفَتَأۡتُونَ ٱلسِّحۡرَ وَأَنتُمۡ تُبۡصِرُونَ
transliteration: Lahiyatan quloobuhum waasarroo alnnajwa allatheena thalamoo hal hatha illa basharun mithlukum afatatoona alssihra waantum tubsiroona
translation: Just to serve as a distraction for their minds. And the wicked people secretly whisper, “Is he not just a man like you? Will you then knowingly yield to the magic spell?”
notes: This part of the Verse is a complement to Verse 2 above.

arabic: قَالَ رَبِّي يَعۡلَمُ ٱلۡقَوۡلَ فِي ٱلسَّمَآءِ وَٱلۡأَرۡضِۖ وَهُوَ ٱلسَّمِيعُ ٱلۡعَلِيمُ
transliteration: Qala rabbee yaAAlamu alqawla fee alssamai waalardi wahuwa alssameeAAu alAAaleemu
translation: The Prophet said, “My Lord knows every word spoken anywhere in the heavens and the earth. And He is the One to hear and the One to know everything.”
notes: The mind of a living creature does need some amusement after performance of the duty allotted to it by its Creator. It is preposterous to think that the Creator Himself would need any amusement! The use of the pronoun in plural, We, in the Verse indicates a divine establishment, with angels working therein carrying out the Creator’s orders. And the angels being themselves creatures may need some amusement. But the Creator need not have made the elaborate creation of the huge universe just for the amusement of the angels; He could have very well arranged for the angels’ amusement within the divine establishment itself.

arabic: بَلۡ قَالُوٓاْ أَضۡغَٰثُ أَحۡلَٰمِۭ بَلِ ٱفۡتَرَىٰهُ بَلۡ هُوَ شَاعِرٞ فَلۡيَأۡتِنَا بِـَٔايَةٖ كَمَآ أُرۡسِلَ ٱلۡأَوَّلُونَ
transliteration: Bal qaloo adghathu ahlamin bali iftarahu bal huwa shaAAirun falyatina biayatin kama orsila alawwaloona
translation: But they variously said, “Just a confusing mixture of dreams!” “But he has forged it.” “But he is a poet.” “Let him then bring to us a sign just as the earlier prophets were sent with.”

arabic: مَآ ءَامَنَتۡ قَبۡلَهُم مِّن قَرۡيَةٍ أَهۡلَكۡنَٰهَآۖ أَفَهُمۡ يُؤۡمِنُونَ
transliteration: Ma amanat qablahum min qaryatin ahlaknaha afahum yuminoona
translation: Any town/village that We had destroyed before them, too, had not believed! Will they, taking this fact into account at least, believe?
notes: Refer study notes 482 & 483 on Verse 2:255 (Manzil I) in this regard.

arabic: وَمَآ أَرۡسَلۡنَا قَبۡلَكَ إِلَّا رِجَالٗا نُّوحِيٓ إِلَيۡهِمۡۖ فَسۡـَٔلُوٓاْ أَهۡلَ ٱلذِّكۡرِ إِن كُنتُمۡ لَا تَعۡلَمُونَ
transliteration: Wama arsalna qablaka illa rijalan noohee ilayhim faisaloo ahla alththikri in kuntum la taAAlamoona
translation: And We sent (as Prophets/Messengers for mankind), before you, none but men inspired with Our Revelation. Ask the people of the Reminder (divinely revealed Book) if you do not know.
notes: Here is yet another sign, for the modern man, to come to the belief that the Qur’aan is from none other than the Creator of this universe and of everything therein. In this Verse, revealed over 1400 years ago, it is abundantly made clear that water is the distinguishing feature between the earth and all other heavenly bodies! The earth was as dead as all the other bodies in the beginning, but the Creater first created water therein for His subsequent creation of a myriad of living creatures there from. HE thus distinguished the earth from the other heavenly bodies. The facts mentioned in this Verse stand verified by modern science. It is only recently that some traces of water have been discovered in the moon, but it is far from sufficient for sustaining life. 1400 years ago people were unaware of these facts, which could only have been inserted in the Qur’aan then by none other than the Creator Himself. This Verse moreover lends support to scientists’ ‘big bang’ theory.

arabic: وَمَا جَعَلۡنَٰهُمۡ جَسَدٗا لَّا يَأۡكُلُونَ ٱلطَّعَامَ وَمَا كَانُواْ خَٰلِدِينَ
transliteration: Wama jaAAalnahum jasadan la yakuloona alttaAAama wama kanoo khalideena
translation: And We had not made them bodies that did not eat food, and they were not immortal.
notes: Imagine the earth without its mountains – without its ups and downs. What would happen to its reservoirs of water – the oceans, seas, lakes, ponds etc.? Wouldn’t they spread all over and leave no dry space for humans and animals living on dry land? The fact of the matter is that these mountains, hills and other ups and downs on the surface of the earth give the earth its stability and enability to sustain life as we know it. And the fact is: The Creator has created everything very meticulously. And, yet, most of mankind do not give Him due recognition – to their own detriment, of course.

arabic: ثُمَّ صَدَقۡنَٰهُمُ ٱلۡوَعۡدَ فَأَنجَيۡنَٰهُمۡ وَمَن نَّشَآءُ وَأَهۡلَكۡنَا ٱلۡمُسۡرِفِينَ
transliteration: Thumma sadaqnahumu alwaAAda faanjaynahum waman nashao waahlakna almusrifeena
translation: And We made Our promise good to them; We saved them and those whom We pleased, and We destroyed the transgressors.
notes: It is only now that science has discovered the protective role played by the atmosphere around the earth. The harmful radiations from the Sun and outer space are filtered out therein letting only beneficial sunlight pass through. The temperature on the surface of the earth is maintained within tolerable limits. Water vapour is not allowed to dissipate beyond and thus lost. The water cycle is maintained and sustained. In short, no life would be possible on earth without the protective shield of its atmosphere. [Source] But the Qur’aan had revealed this role centuries before science could discover it. And yet people believe not in Allah and the Qur’aan, His own Book of guidance!

arabic: لَقَدۡ أَنزَلۡنَآ إِلَيۡكُمۡ كِتَٰبٗا فِيهِ ذِكۡرُكُمۡۚ أَفَلَا تَعۡقِلُونَ
transliteration: Laqad anzalna ilaykum kitaban feehi thikrukum afala taAAqiloona
translation: Verily We have revealed to you a Book in which are things for you to ponder over, remember and abide by. Do you then not use your intelligence to understand it?
notes: Muslims did just that during the first few centuries after the revelation of the Qur’aan and climbed to pinnacles of glory in all constructive fields of life. But thereafter they lapsed into negligence towards the divine Book and fell to depths of ignominy as of now.

arabic: وَكَمۡ قَصَمۡنَا مِن قَرۡيَةٖ كَانَتۡ ظَالِمَةٗ وَأَنشَأۡنَا بَعۡدَهَا قَوۡمًا ءَاخَرِينَ
transliteration: Wakam qasamna min qaryatin kanat thalimatan waanshana baAAdaha qawman akhareena
translation: And many a town/village, whose inhabitants were cruel, did We shatter, and We raised up, thereafter, another people!
notes: Earlier peoples, like those of Noah and Lot, got destroyed. The kuffar of Makkah had scoffed at Islam when it was first propounded to them, but Islam overwhelmed them in the end.

arabic: فَلَمَّآ أَحَسُّواْ بَأۡسَنَآ إِذَا هُم مِّنۡهَا يَرۡكُضُونَ
transliteration: Falamma ahassoo basana itha hum minha yarkudoona
translation: No sooner did they perceive Our punishment coming than they began to flee there from.
notes: We had witnessed such a scenario on TV screens in the recent past when huge tsunami waves had hit the shores in the south-east Asian countries. People on the shores watching the waves coming inexorably on towards them ran helter-skelter for shelter. But the waves overtook many of them.

arabic: لَا تَرۡكُضُواْ وَٱرۡجِعُوٓاْ إِلَىٰ مَآ أُتۡرِفۡتُمۡ فِيهِ وَمَسَٰكِنِكُمۡ لَعَلَّكُمۡ تُسۡـَٔلُونَ
transliteration: La tarkudoo wairjiAAoo ila ma otriftum feehi wamasakinikum laAAallakum tusaloona
translation: Flee not and come back to the affluence you were accustomed to live in and to your homes for being questioned!
notes: From the context it appears that the question posed in this Verse is more particularly to the Jews. They had in their hands the Torah, revealed to Moses, in which prediction had already been made about the last Prophet to come with the last divine Message. The Jews and the Christians, therefore, ought to have been among the first ones to believe in this new Message of the Qur’aan. Would they then yet deny it?

arabic: قَالُواْ يَٰوَيۡلَنَآ إِنَّا كُنَّا ظَٰلِمِينَ
transliteration: Qaloo ya waylana inna kunna thalimeena
translation: They said, “O woe to us! We were wicked.”
notes: Abraham had obviously said this in undertones, out of hearing of the polytheists. He wanted to demonstrate to them that the statues they worshipped had no power even to save themselves, once they were devoid the protection of their worshippers.

arabic: فَمَا زَالَت تِّلۡكَ دَعۡوَىٰهُمۡ حَتَّىٰ جَعَلۡنَٰهُمۡ حَصِيدًا خَٰمِدِينَ
transliteration: Fama zalat tilka daAAwahum hatta jaAAalnahum haseedan khamideena
translation: And that cry of theirs continued till We made them like smouldering harvested field.
notes: Abraham demonstrated to his people – by breaking the idols – that their so-called gods were incapable of protecting themselves, let alone protecting their worshippers. Allah has given mankind a higher level of reasoning power, especially to enable them to recognize the existence of One Super Power controlling the entire universe and everything therein. And Abraham’s people did not even use their reasoning power to understand the utter futility of their worshipping the lifeless stone idols! Abraham used his intellect to know Allah Almighty. The Qur’aan tells its followers to follow Abraham’s creed. Muslims should then use their intellect to know the Almighty. Most of them are instead wasting their time in going after the shadow of imagined miraculous deeds of dead human khwajas, dast-geers, mushkil-kushas and what not! They are being as unintelligent as those ancient people of Abraham. Isn’t Allah virtually telling them, ‘Fie on you!’ when they have now become favourite whipping boys for any Tom, Dick or Harry in this modern world?

arabic: وَمَا خَلَقۡنَا ٱلسَّمَآءَ وَٱلۡأَرۡضَ وَمَا بَيۡنَهُمَا لَٰعِبِينَ
transliteration: Wama khalaqna alssamaa waalarda wama baynahuma laAAibeena
translation: And We did not create the heavens and the earth, and what is between them, for fun.
notes: The other Verses (7:137, 17:1, 21:81, 34:18) mentioning the land being divinely blessed do indicate that it is Palestine and the places around, once ruled over by King Solomon. This area had been the bone of contention for Mulims and the Christians during the Crusades earlier. And now it is the bone of contention between the Muslims and the Jews. The Qur’aan tells us that it is a blessed land. The blessings were to be seen only during Solomon’s rein. The land is still potentially blessed; but the blessings are frittered away by the intransigent and warring people, who fail to abide by divine law.

arabic: لَوۡ أَرَدۡنَآ أَن نَّتَّخِذَ لَهۡوٗا لَّٱتَّخَذۡنَٰهُ مِن لَّدُنَّآ إِن كُنَّا فَٰعِلِينَ
transliteration: Law aradna an nattakhitha lahwan laittakhathnahu min ladunna in kunna faAAileena
translation: Had We wished to have some amusement, We would have derived it from within Ourselves, if at all.
notes: The mind of a living creature does need some amusement after performance of the duty allotted to it by its Creator. It is preposterous to think that the Creator Himself would need any amusement! The use of the pronoun in plural, We, in the Verse indicates a divine establishment, with angels working therein carrying out the Creator’s orders. And the angels being themselves creatures may need some amusement. But the Creator need not have made the elaborate creation of the huge universe just for the amusement of the angels; He could have very well arranged for the angels’ amusement within the divine establishment itself.

arabic: بَلۡ نَقۡذِفُ بِٱلۡحَقِّ عَلَى ٱلۡبَٰطِلِ فَيَدۡمَغُهُۥ فَإِذَا هُوَ زَاهِقٞۚ وَلَكُمُ ٱلۡوَيۡلُ مِمَّا تَصِفُونَ
transliteration: Bal naqthifu bialhaqqi AAala albatili fayadmaghuhu faitha huwa zahiqun walakumu alwaylu mimma tasifoona
translation: Nay! We cast the truth against the falsehood to demolish it (falsehood). And lo! Falsehood perishes. And woe to you for the (wrong) way you perceive things.
notes: Solomon was King David’s son. Although the details of the case in question are not given, what we can surmise from the context given is that there was an unauthorized pasturing, on a certain person’s field, by the sheep of others. The matter was brought before King David for decision. The King gave his decision, but son Solomon, who was present at the hearing, suggested a different decision. From the beginning part of the next Verse 79, it is clear, that Solomon’s suggestion was better, and it was ultimately accepted by the King. This episode is narrated here to highlight the sagacity and magnanimity of King David in recognizing and accepting a better decision, though it was at variance with his own and was given by a mere boy. In the ultimate analysis, it is Allah Who puts the right decision in the mind of whomsoever He pleases. Any person concerned should have the large-heartedness to recognize and accept the right decision even when it comes from someone else. It is this attitude that promotes welfare and harmony in public relations.

arabic: وَلَهُۥ مَن فِي ٱلسَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَٱلۡأَرۡضِۚ وَمَنۡ عِندَهُۥ لَا يَسۡتَكۡبِرُونَ عَنۡ عِبَادَتِهِۦ وَلَا يَسۡتَحۡسِرُونَ
transliteration: Walahu man fee alssamawati waalardi waman AAindahu la yastakbiroona AAan AAibadatihi wala yastahsiroona
translation: And whoever is in the heavens and the earth is His. And those who are with Him are never too proud to worship Him, nor do they get weary.
notes: I.e. Zaboor inspired to David (see Verse 4:163).

arabic: يُسَبِّحُونَ ٱلَّيۡلَ وَٱلنَّهَارَ لَا يَفۡتُرُونَ
transliteration: Yusabbihoona allayla waalnnahara la yafturoona
translation: They glorify Allah, night and day, without a break.
notes: Every innovation – like the armours inspired to David in the ancient past – that has facilitated the ever-increasing ease and convenience in any field of human activity is inspired by Allah Almighty, the real Innovator of all things. HE had inspired David. HE had inspired several Muslims to invent many things that heralded the renaissance of the modern age. And since the Muslims ungratefully consigned the Qur’aan for just ritual reading for occasions like death anniversaries etc., he inspired non-Muslims to make the innovations. But all the innovations are in fact His! But man, in his undeserved arrogance, thinks that it is he who has done it all by dint of his brilliant intellect. He forgets that there was a time when he was nothing. He forgets that his life started as an infinitesimal speck of matter. He forgets that it was not his brilliant brain that fashioned him (including his brain to which he gives credit for all the innovations). He is utterly and shamelessly ungrateful to his Unseen Maker.

arabic: أَمِ ٱتَّخَذُوٓاْ ءَالِهَةٗ مِّنَ ٱلۡأَرۡضِ هُمۡ يُنشِرُونَ
transliteration: Ami ittakhathoo alihatan mina alardi hum yunshiroona
translation: Or have they taken gods whom they themselves have fashioned/designed out of the earth!?
notes: Job (Ayyub) was one of the numerous Prophets of Allah. Not much has been stated about him in the Qur’aan except for what we find in these 2 Verses (83 & 84) here and in Verses 38:41 to 38:44. And from the information given, we see that Job was the very epitome of the virtue of patience. He had suffered much – in wealth, health and family – perhaps more than what any other person has ever suffered. But he never lost his faith in Allah. And in the end, he got back what he had lost. The Lord was just preparing him to be set up as patience exemplified.

arabic: لَوۡ كَانَ فِيهِمَآ ءَالِهَةٌ إِلَّا ٱللَّهُ لَفَسَدَتَاۚ فَسُبۡحَٰنَ ٱللَّهِ رَبِّ ٱلۡعَرۡشِ عَمَّا يَصِفُونَ
transliteration: Law kana feehima alihatun illa Allahu lafasadata fasubhana Allahi rabbi alAAarshi AAamma yasifoona
translation: Had there been in them any gods other than Allah, the two (the heavens and the earth) would certainly have been in a state of disorder. Glorified then is Allah, the Lord of the Throne , above all that they attribute to Him.

arabic: لَا يُسۡـَٔلُ عَمَّا يَفۡعَلُ وَهُمۡ يُسۡـَٔلُونَ
transliteration: La yusalu AAamma yafAAalu wahum yusaloona
translation: He cannot be questioned on what He does, and they (human beings) shall be questioned on what they do!
notes: Verse 38:48 has the only other reference to this Prophet.

arabic: أَمِ ٱتَّخَذُواْ مِن دُونِهِۦٓ ءَالِهَةٗۖ قُلۡ هَاتُواْ بُرۡهَٰنَكُمۡۖ هَٰذَا ذِكۡرُ مَن مَّعِيَ وَذِكۡرُ مَن قَبۡلِيۚ بَلۡ أَكۡثَرُهُمۡ لَا يَعۡلَمُونَ ٱلۡحَقَّۖ فَهُم مُّعۡرِضُونَ
transliteration: Ami ittakhathoo min doonihi alihatan qul hatoo burhanakum hatha thikru man maAAiya wathikru man qablee bal aktharuhum la yaAAlamoona alhaqqa fahum muAAridoona
translation: Or, have they taken gods other than Him? Say, “Produce your evidence! This is the precept (that there is no god besides Allah) for those with me and the precept for those before me.” But most of them do not know the truth, and so they are intransigent.
notes: Verses 37:139 to 37:148 inform us that it was Prophet Jonas (Yunus) who had been swallowed by a big fish. So we can infer here that the epithet ‘man of the fish’ refers to him.

arabic: وَمَآ أَرۡسَلۡنَا مِن قَبۡلِكَ مِن رَّسُولٍ إِلَّا نُوحِيٓ إِلَيۡهِ أَنَّهُۥ لَآ إِلَٰهَ إِلَّآ أَنَا۠ فَٱعۡبُدُونِ
transliteration: Wama arsalna min qablika min rasoolin illa noohee ilayhi annahu la ilaha illa ana faoAAbudooni
translation: And We did not send before you any Messenger, but We revealed to him that there is no god but I, and so worship Me.
notes: We learn, from information given in other parts of the Qur’aan, that Jonas had abandoned his people, before Allah permitted him, because they would not believe in him.

arabic: وَقَالُواْ ٱتَّخَذَ ٱلرَّحۡمَٰنُ وَلَدٗاۗ سُبۡحَٰنَهُۥۚ بَلۡ عِبَادٞ مُّكۡرَمُونَ
transliteration: Waqaloo ittakhatha alrrahmanu waladan subhanahu bal AAibadun mukramoona
translation: And they say, “The Gracious One has begotten (angels)!” Glorified is He. Nay! They are His honoured worshippers.
notes: See Verses 19:2 to 19:15 for further details.

arabic: لَا يَسۡبِقُونَهُۥ بِٱلۡقَوۡلِ وَهُم بِأَمۡرِهِۦ يَعۡمَلُونَ
transliteration: La yasbiqoonahu bialqawli wahum biamrihi yaAAmaloona
translation: They do not speak ahead of Him and they act at His Command.
notes: The Qur’aan uses this expression for activation of every human life right from Adam (Verses 32.7 to 32.9). With our advanced knowledge of embryonic growth, we may now say that this activation takes place, at about the end of the 3rd week of conception, when the rudimentary heart of the tiny speck of life inside a womb starts beating. So this Qur’aanic expression should not be misconstrued to substantiate the Christians’ preposterous claim that Jesus was son of God.

arabic: يَعۡلَمُ مَا بَيۡنَ أَيۡدِيهِمۡ وَمَا خَلۡفَهُمۡ وَلَا يَشۡفَعُونَ إِلَّا لِمَنِ ٱرۡتَضَىٰ وَهُم مِّنۡ خَشۡيَتِهِۦ مُشۡفِقُونَ
transliteration: YaAAlamu ma bayna aydeehim wama khalfahum wala yashfaAAoona illa limani irtada wahum min khashyatihi mushfiqoona
translation: HE knows what they know of and what they do not know of. And they intercede not except for him whom He wills and they are on their guard in awe of Him.
notes: Refer study notes 482 & 483 on Verse 2:255 (Manzil I) in this regard.

arabic: ۞وَمَن يَقُلۡ مِنۡهُمۡ إِنِّيٓ إِلَٰهٞ مِّن دُونِهِۦ فَذَٰلِكَ نَجۡزِيهِ جَهَنَّمَۚ كَذَٰلِكَ نَجۡزِي ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ
transliteration: Waman yaqul minhum innee ilahun min doonihi fathalika najzeehi jahannama kathalika najzee alththalimeena
translation: And whoever of them says that he is indeed a god besides Him, We award Hell to such a one. We do thus award the wicked people.
notes: I.e. the community that submits to Allah Almighty (refer Verse 2:128 [Manzil I]). Its members may be living in any part of the world, but they all belong to the same single community as declared here by the Creator Himself.

arabic: أَوَلَمۡ يَرَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوٓاْ أَنَّ ٱلسَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَٱلۡأَرۡضَ كَانَتَا رَتۡقٗا فَفَتَقۡنَٰهُمَاۖ وَجَعَلۡنَا مِنَ ٱلۡمَآءِ كُلَّ شَيۡءٍ حَيٍّۚ أَفَلَا يُؤۡمِنُونَ
transliteration: Awalam yara allatheena kafaroo anna alssamawati waalarda kanata ratqan fafataqnahuma wajaAAalna mina almai kulla shayin hayyin afala yuminoona
translation: Do those who suppress the Truth not see that the heavens and the earth were both one joint entity, and then We tore them apart. And We have made of water everything living, will they not then believe?
notes: Here is yet another sign, for the modern man, to come to the belief that the Qur’aan is from none other than the Creator of this universe and of everything therein. In this Verse, revealed over 1400 years ago, it is abundantly made clear that water is the distinguishing feature between the earth and all other heavenly bodies! The earth was as dead as all the other bodies in the beginning, but the Creater first created water therein for His subsequent creation of a myriad of living creatures there from. HE thus distinguished the earth from the other heavenly bodies. The facts mentioned in this Verse stand verified by modern science. It is only recently that some traces of water have been discovered in the moon, but it is far from sufficient for sustaining life. 1400 years ago people were unaware of these facts, which could only have been inserted in the Qur’aan then by none other than the Creator Himself. This Verse moreover lends support to scientists’ ‘big bang’ theory.

arabic: وَجَعَلۡنَا فِي ٱلۡأَرۡضِ رَوَٰسِيَ أَن تَمِيدَ بِهِمۡ وَجَعَلۡنَا فِيهَا فِجَاجٗا سُبُلٗا لَّعَلَّهُمۡ يَهۡتَدُونَ
transliteration: WajaAAalna fee alardi rawasiya an tameeda bihim wajaAAalna feeha fijajan subulan laAAallahum yahtadoona
translation: And We have placed mountains in the earth so that positions thereon do not move. And We have made therein passes to enable people to move about and find their way.
notes: Imagine the earth without its mountains – without its ups and downs. What would happen to its reservoirs of water – the oceans, seas, lakes, ponds etc.? Wouldn’t they spread all over and leave no dry space for humans and animals living on dry land? The fact of the matter is that these mountains, hills and other ups and downs on the surface of the earth give the earth its stability and enability to sustain life as we know it. And the fact is: The Creator has created everything very meticulously. And, yet, most of mankind do not give Him due recognition – to their own detriment, of course.

arabic: وَجَعَلۡنَا ٱلسَّمَآءَ سَقۡفٗا مَّحۡفُوظٗاۖ وَهُمۡ عَنۡ ءَايَٰتِهَا مُعۡرِضُونَ
transliteration: WajaAAalna alssamaa saqfan mahfoothan wahum AAan ayatiha muAAridoona
translation: And We have made the sky a protective roof and yet they are oblivious to its signs!
notes: It is only now that science has discovered the protective role played by the atmosphere around the earth. The harmful radiations from the Sun and outer space are filtered out therein letting only beneficial sunlight pass through. The temperature on the surface of the earth is maintained within tolerable limits. Water vapour is not allowed to dissipate beyond and thus lost. The water cycle is maintained and sustained. In short, no life would be possible on earth without the protective shield of its atmosphere. [Source] But the Qur’aan had revealed this role centuries before science could discover it. And yet people believe not in Allah and the Qur’aan, His own Book of guidance!

arabic: وَهُوَ ٱلَّذِي خَلَقَ ٱلَّيۡلَ وَٱلنَّهَارَ وَٱلشَّمۡسَ وَٱلۡقَمَرَۖ كُلّٞ فِي فَلَكٖ يَسۡبَحُونَ
transliteration: Wahuwa allathee khalaqa allayla waalnnahara waalshshamsa waalqamara kullun fee falakin yasbahoona
translation: And He it is Who created the night and the day, the sun and the moon. All in orbit float.
notes: Those in Hell shall be deaf because they were deaf in this world to Qur’aanic admonition.

arabic: وَمَا جَعَلۡنَا لِبَشَرٖ مِّن قَبۡلِكَ ٱلۡخُلۡدَۖ أَفَإِيْن مِّتَّ فَهُمُ ٱلۡخَٰلِدُونَ
transliteration: Wama jaAAalna libasharin min qablika alkhulda afain mitta fahumu alkhalidoona
translation: And We did not ordain immortality for any man before you. And so, if you die, will they remain alive?
notes: The Book (Psalms) revealed to David (Verse 4:163 [Manzil I]). “The just inherit shall the land, and ever in it dwell” Psalm No. 37.29. [Source: The Psalms of David in Metre Book 1, Psalms 1-41 ]

arabic: كُلُّ نَفۡسٖ ذَآئِقَةُ ٱلۡمَوۡتِۗ وَنَبۡلُوكُم بِٱلشَّرِّ وَٱلۡخَيۡرِ فِتۡنَةٗۖ وَإِلَيۡنَا تُرۡجَعُونَ
transliteration: Kullu nafsin thaiqatu almawti wanablookum bialshsharri waalkhayri fitnatan wailayna turjaAAoona
translation: Everyone must taste death. And We test you with trial periods of adversity and prosperity. And to Us you shall be brought back.
notes: The 2nd person singular pronoun used here is obviously for the Prophet (peace on him). Now, why has he been described as a mercy for the worlds? As for the interpretation of ‘worlds’ (aalameen), please see study note 5 under Verse 1:2. We may therefore infer that the Prophet was sent as a mercy for every individual human life. In what sense was he a mercy? He was Allah’s mercy in the sense that he conveyed to us the Creator’s Own Manual (the Qur’aan) for right conduct of human life. Without this Manual, every man/woman would be just groping in the dark, not knowing what path to follow in this labyrinthine world. And how does one know that the Qur’aanic path is the right path? The distinguishing feature is: the Qur’aan contains no contradictions, and it is inimitable. It has stood this test for over 1400 years now.

arabic: وَإِذَا رَءَاكَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوٓاْ إِن يَتَّخِذُونَكَ إِلَّا هُزُوًا أَهَٰذَا ٱلَّذِي يَذۡكُرُ ءَالِهَتَكُمۡ وَهُم بِذِكۡرِ ٱلرَّحۡمَٰنِ هُمۡ كَٰفِرُونَ
transliteration: Waitha raaka allatheena kafaroo in yattakhithoonaka illa huzuwan ahatha allathee yathkuru alihatakum wahum bithikri alrrahmani hum kafiroona
translation: And when those who suppress the Truth see you, they do not take you but for one to be scoffed at, “Is this the one who talks of your gods?” And they suppress mention of the Gracious One.

arabic: خُلِقَ ٱلۡإِنسَٰنُ مِنۡ عَجَلٖۚ سَأُوْرِيكُمۡ ءَايَٰتِي فَلَا تَسۡتَعۡجِلُونِ
transliteration: Khuliqa alinsanu min AAajalin saoreekum ayatee fala tastaAAjiloona
translation: Man is created hasty in nature. Soon shall I show you My signs. So be not hasty!
notes: And surely enough, Allah Almighty has shown quite a many sign, in the Qur’aan as also in Nature, that He exists and that the Qur’aan is the Book of Guidance from none else but He. The immediately preceding study notes 8 to 10 herein above describe 3 of the numerous such signs that we have come across so far during these Studies of the Qur’aan.

arabic: وَيَقُولُونَ مَتَىٰ هَٰذَا ٱلۡوَعۡدُ إِن كُنتُمۡ صَٰدِقِينَ
transliteration: Wayaqooloona mata hatha alwaAAdu in kuntum sadiqeena
translation: And they ask, “When will this prediction come to pass if what you say is true?”

arabic: لَوۡ يَعۡلَمُ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُواْ حِينَ لَا يَكُفُّونَ عَن وُجُوهِهِمُ ٱلنَّارَ وَلَا عَن ظُهُورِهِمۡ وَلَا هُمۡ يُنصَرُونَ
transliteration: Law yaAAlamu allatheena kafaroo heena la yakuffoona AAan wujoohihimu alnnara wala AAan thuhoorihim wala hum yunsaroona
translation: Had those who suppress the Truth but known that a time would come when they shall not be able to ward off the fire from their faces or from their backs! And then they shall get no help.

arabic: بَلۡ تَأۡتِيهِم بَغۡتَةٗ فَتَبۡهَتُهُمۡ فَلَا يَسۡتَطِيعُونَ رَدَّهَا وَلَا هُمۡ يُنظَرُونَ
transliteration: Bal tateehim baghtatan fatabhatuhum fala yastateeAAoona raddaha wala hum yuntharoona
translation: But it (the time mentioned in Verse 39 above) shall come on them suddenly and catch them unawares. And they shall not be able to avert it. And they shall not be given any respite.

arabic: وَلَقَدِ ٱسۡتُهۡزِئَ بِرُسُلٖ مِّن قَبۡلِكَ فَحَاقَ بِٱلَّذِينَ سَخِرُواْ مِنۡهُم مَّا كَانُواْ بِهِۦ يَسۡتَهۡزِءُونَ
transliteration: Walaqadi istuhzia birusulin min qablika fahaqa biallatheena sakhiroo minhum ma kanoo bihi yastahzioona
translation: And certainly Messengers before you were scoffed at. But then the thing they mocked about surrounded those of them who scoffed.
notes: Earlier peoples, like those of Noah and Lot, got destroyed. The kuffar of Makkah had scoffed at Islam when it was first propounded to them, but Islam overwhelmed them in the end.

arabic: قُلۡ مَن يَكۡلَؤُكُم بِٱلَّيۡلِ وَٱلنَّهَارِ مِنَ ٱلرَّحۡمَٰنِۚ بَلۡ هُمۡ عَن ذِكۡرِ رَبِّهِم مُّعۡرِضُونَ
transliteration: Qul man yaklaokum biallayli waalnnahari mina alrrahmani bal hum AAan thikri rabbihim muAAridoona
translation: Ask them, “Who can guard you, by night and by day, from the Gracious One?” And, yet, they turn away at the mention of their Lord.

arabic: أَمۡ لَهُمۡ ءَالِهَةٞ تَمۡنَعُهُم مِّن دُونِنَاۚ لَا يَسۡتَطِيعُونَ نَصۡرَ أَنفُسِهِمۡ وَلَا هُم مِّنَّا يُصۡحَبُونَ
transliteration: Am lahum alihatun tamnaAAuhum min doonina la yastateeAAoona nasra anfusihim wala hum minna yushaboona
translation: Or, have they gods who can defend them against Us? They shall not be able to help themselves. Nor shall they have any benefit of association with Us.

arabic: بَلۡ مَتَّعۡنَا هَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ وَءَابَآءَهُمۡ حَتَّىٰ طَالَ عَلَيۡهِمُ ٱلۡعُمُرُۗ أَفَلَا يَرَوۡنَ أَنَّا نَأۡتِي ٱلۡأَرۡضَ نَنقُصُهَا مِنۡ أَطۡرَافِهَآۚ أَفَهُمُ ٱلۡغَٰلِبُونَ
transliteration: Bal mattaAAna haolai waabaahum hatta tala AAalayhimu alAAumuru afala yarawna anna natee alarda nanqusuha min atrafiha afahumu alghaliboona
translation: But, for a prolonged period in their lives, We did provide for them and their fathers with good things of life. Have they therefore become so complacent as not to see that We are closing in on them from all sides? And does their complacency make them think that they shall still prevail?

arabic: قُلۡ إِنَّمَآ أُنذِرُكُم بِٱلۡوَحۡيِۚ وَلَا يَسۡمَعُ ٱلصُّمُّ ٱلدُّعَآءَ إِذَا مَا يُنذَرُونَ
transliteration: Qul innama onthirukum bialwahyi wala yasmaAAu alssummu aldduAAaa itha ma yuntharoona
translation: Tell them, “I warn you only because I have been divinely instructed to do so.” And the deaf do not hear the call howsoever much they are warned.

arabic: وَلَئِن مَّسَّتۡهُمۡ نَفۡحَةٞ مِّنۡ عَذَابِ رَبِّكَ لَيَقُولُنَّ يَٰوَيۡلَنَآ إِنَّا كُنَّا ظَٰلِمِينَ
transliteration: Walain massathum nafhatun min AAathabi rabbika layaqoolunna ya waylana inna kunna thalimeena
translation: And if punishment of your Lord were to touch them even slightly, they would certainly say, “O woe to us! We did indeed commit wrong.”

arabic: وَنَضَعُ ٱلۡمَوَٰزِينَ ٱلۡقِسۡطَ لِيَوۡمِ ٱلۡقِيَٰمَةِ فَلَا تُظۡلَمُ نَفۡسٞ شَيۡـٔٗاۖ وَإِن كَانَ مِثۡقَالَ حَبَّةٖ مِّنۡ خَرۡدَلٍ أَتَيۡنَا بِهَاۗ وَكَفَىٰ بِنَا حَٰسِبِينَ
transliteration: WanadaAAu almawazeena alqista liyawmi alqiyamati fala tuthlamu nafsun shayan wain kana mithqala habbatin min khardalin atayna biha wakafa bina hasibeena
translation: And We will set up just scales for the Day of Resurrection, so no wrong shall be done to anyone. And if there be anything as little in weight as that of a mustard seed, We will present it nevertheless. And We are sufficiently proficient in keeping accounts.

arabic: وَلَقَدۡ ءَاتَيۡنَا مُوسَىٰ وَهَٰرُونَ ٱلۡفُرۡقَانَ وَضِيَآءٗ وَذِكۡرٗا لِّلۡمُتَّقِينَ
transliteration: Walaqad atayna moosa waharoona alfurqana wadiyaan wathikran lilmuttaqeena
translation: And certainly We did give Moses and Aaron the Criterion, a Light, and a Book often to refer to for guidance to those who would fear Allah and be pious.
notes: As the Qur’aan is the Criterion, the Light and Zikr for the entire mankind since its revelation, so was the Torah, for the Children of Israel, till the Injeel was revealed for them.

arabic: ٱلَّذِينَ يَخۡشَوۡنَ رَبَّهُم بِٱلۡغَيۡبِ وَهُم مِّنَ ٱلسَّاعَةِ مُشۡفِقُونَ
transliteration: Allatheena yakhshawna rabbahum bialghaybi wahum mina alssaAAati mushfiqoona
translation: Those that feared their Lord without seeing Him and were anxiously concerned about the Hour (marking the end of this world and heralding the Day of Judgment in the Hereafter).

arabic: وَهَٰذَا ذِكۡرٞ مُّبَارَكٌ أَنزَلۡنَٰهُۚ أَفَأَنتُمۡ لَهُۥ مُنكِرُونَ
transliteration: Wahatha thikrun mubarakun anzalnahu afaantum lahu munkiroona
translation: And would you then deny that this (Qur’aan) is the blessed Book, often to refer to for guidance, We have now revealed?
notes: From the context it appears that the question posed in this Verse is more particularly to the Jews. They had in their hands the Torah, revealed to Moses, in which prediction had already been made about the last Prophet to come with the last divine Message. The Jews and the Christians, therefore, ought to have been among the first ones to believe in this new Message of the Qur’aan. Would they then yet deny it?

arabic: ۞وَلَقَدۡ ءَاتَيۡنَآ إِبۡرَٰهِيمَ رُشۡدَهُۥ مِن قَبۡلُ وَكُنَّا بِهِۦ عَٰلِمِينَ
transliteration: Walaqad atayna ibraheema rushdahu min qablu wakunna bihi AAalimeena
translation: And certainly We did give Abraham, before (Moses and Aaron), his firmly right conduct. And We were aware of this

arabic: إِذۡ قَالَ لِأَبِيهِ وَقَوۡمِهِۦ مَا هَٰذِهِ ٱلتَّمَاثِيلُ ٱلَّتِيٓ أَنتُمۡ لَهَا عَٰكِفُونَ
transliteration: Ith qala liabeehi waqawmihi ma hathihi alttamatheelu allatee antum laha AAakifoona
translation: When he asked his father and his people, “What statues are these that you are so devoted to?”

arabic: قَالُواْ وَجَدۡنَآ ءَابَآءَنَا لَهَا عَٰبِدِينَ
transliteration: Qaloo wajadna abaana laha AAabideena
translation: They said, “We found our fathers worshipping them.”

arabic: قَالَ لَقَدۡ كُنتُمۡ أَنتُمۡ وَءَابَآؤُكُمۡ فِي ضَلَٰلٖ مُّبِينٖ
transliteration: Qala laqad kuntum antum waabaokum fee dalalin mubeenin
translation: Abraham said, “Certainly you had – you and your fathers – gone far astray.”

arabic: قَالُوٓاْ أَجِئۡتَنَا بِٱلۡحَقِّ أَمۡ أَنتَ مِنَ ٱللَّـٰعِبِينَ
transliteration: Qaloo ajitana bialhaqqi am anta mina allaAAibeena
translation: They said, “Are you telling us the truth, or just making fun of us?”

arabic: قَالَ بَل رَّبُّكُمۡ رَبُّ ٱلسَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَٱلۡأَرۡضِ ٱلَّذِي فَطَرَهُنَّ وَأَنَا۠ عَلَىٰ ذَٰلِكُم مِّنَ ٱلشَّـٰهِدِينَ
transliteration: Qala bal rabbukum rabbu alssamawati waalardi allathee fatarahunna waana AAala thalikum mina alshshahideena
translation: He said, “But your Lord is the Lord of the heavens and the earth. It is He Who created them out of nothing. And I am of those who bear witness to this.”

arabic: وَتَٱللَّهِ لَأَكِيدَنَّ أَصۡنَٰمَكُم بَعۡدَ أَن تُوَلُّواْ مُدۡبِرِينَ
transliteration: WataAllahi laakeedanna asnamakum baAAda an tuwalloo mudbireena
translation: “And, by Allah! I will certainly do something to your idols after you go away, turning your backs.”
notes: Abraham had obviously said this in undertones, out of hearing of the polytheists. He wanted to demonstrate to them that the statues they worshipped had no power even to save themselves, once they were devoid the protection of their worshippers.

arabic: فَجَعَلَهُمۡ جُذَٰذًا إِلَّا كَبِيرٗا لَّهُمۡ لَعَلَّهُمۡ إِلَيۡهِ يَرۡجِعُونَ
transliteration: FajaAAalahum juthathan illa kabeeran lahum laAAallahum ilayhi yarjiAAoona
translation: So he broke those (idols) into pieces, except for the biggest of them, that they (polytheists) may turn to it (to inform them as to who did the destruction of the other idols).

arabic: قَالُواْ مَن فَعَلَ هَٰذَا بِـَٔالِهَتِنَآ إِنَّهُۥ لَمِنَ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ
transliteration: Qaloo man faAAala hatha bialihatina innahu lamina alththalimeena
translation: They asked, “Who has done this to our gods? The one who did this is certainly one of those most wicked!”

arabic: قَالُواْ سَمِعۡنَا فَتٗى يَذۡكُرُهُمۡ يُقَالُ لَهُۥٓ إِبۡرَٰهِيمُ
transliteration: Qaloo samiAAna fatan yathkuruhum yuqalu lahu ibraheemu
translation: Some said, “We had heard a youth called Abraham speaking about them.”

arabic: قَالُواْ فَأۡتُواْ بِهِۦ عَلَىٰٓ أَعۡيُنِ ٱلنَّاسِ لَعَلَّهُمۡ يَشۡهَدُونَ
transliteration: Qaloo fatoo bihi AAala aAAyuni alnnasi laAAallahum yashhadoona
translation: The others said, “Then bring him before the eyes of the people. Let all witness.”

arabic: قَالُوٓاْ ءَأَنتَ فَعَلۡتَ هَٰذَا بِـَٔالِهَتِنَا يَـٰٓإِبۡرَٰهِيمُ
transliteration: Qaloo aanta faAAalta hatha bialihatina ya ibraheemu
translation: The polytheists said, “Have you done this to our gods, O Abraham!?”

arabic: قَالَ بَلۡ فَعَلَهُۥ كَبِيرُهُمۡ هَٰذَا فَسۡـَٔلُوهُمۡ إِن كَانُواْ يَنطِقُونَ
transliteration: Qala bal faAAalahu kabeeruhum hatha faisaloohum in kanoo yantiqoona
translation: Abraham said, “Nay, it is this one – the chief of them – that has done it. So, ask them, if they can speak coherently!”

arabic: فَرَجَعُوٓاْ إِلَىٰٓ أَنفُسِهِمۡ فَقَالُوٓاْ إِنَّكُمۡ أَنتُمُ ٱلظَّـٰلِمُونَ
transliteration: FarajaAAoo ila anfusihim faqaloo innakum antumu alththalimoona
translation: Then, turning to themselves, they said, “You yourselves indeed are in the wrong.”

arabic: ثُمَّ نُكِسُواْ عَلَىٰ رُءُوسِهِمۡ لَقَدۡ عَلِمۡتَ مَا هَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ يَنطِقُونَ
transliteration: Thumma nukisoo AAala ruoosihim laqad AAalimta ma haolai yantiqoona
translation: And, their heads hanging down, they said to Abraham, “You do know they do not speak.”

arabic: قَالَ أَفَتَعۡبُدُونَ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ مَا لَا يَنفَعُكُمۡ شَيۡـٔٗا وَلَا يَضُرُّكُمۡ
transliteration: Qala afataAAbudoona min dooni Allahi ma la yanfaAAukum shayan wala yadurrukum
translation: Abraham said, “Do you then worship, besides Allah, that which brings you no benefit at all, and no harm either?”

arabic: أُفّٖ لَّكُمۡ وَلِمَا تَعۡبُدُونَ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِۚ أَفَلَا تَعۡقِلُونَ
transliteration: Offin lakum walima taAAbudoona min dooni Allahi afala taAAqiloona
translation: “Fie on you and on that which you worship besides Allah! Why do you not use your intellect?”
notes: Abraham demonstrated to his people – by breaking the idols – that their so-called gods were incapable of protecting themselves, let alone protecting their worshippers. Allah has given mankind a higher level of reasoning power, especially to enable them to recognize the existence of One Super Power controlling the entire universe and everything therein. And Abraham’s people did not even use their reasoning power to understand the utter futility of their worshipping the lifeless stone idols! Abraham used his intellect to know Allah Almighty. The Qur’aan tells its followers to follow Abraham’s creed. Muslims should then use their intellect to know the Almighty. Most of them are instead wasting their time in going after the shadow of imagined miraculous deeds of dead human khwajas, dast-geers, mushkil-kushas and what not! They are being as unintelligent as those ancient people of Abraham. Isn’t Allah virtually telling them, ‘Fie on you!’ when they have now become favourite whipping boys for any Tom, Dick or Harry in this modern world?

arabic: قَالُواْ حَرِّقُوهُ وَٱنصُرُوٓاْ ءَالِهَتَكُمۡ إِن كُنتُمۡ فَٰعِلِينَ
transliteration: Qaloo harriqoohu waonsuroo alihatakum in kuntum faAAileena
translation: They said, “Put him in the fire and help your gods, if you are going to do anything.”

arabic: قُلۡنَا يَٰنَارُ كُونِي بَرۡدٗا وَسَلَٰمًا عَلَىٰٓ إِبۡرَٰهِيمَ
transliteration: Qulna ya naru koonee bardan wasalaman AAala ibraheema
translation: WE ordered, “O fire! Be you a source of cool comfort and peace on Abraham.” 21:70 And they had designed a plot against him, but We made them themselves the doomed ones.

arabic: وَأَرَادُواْ بِهِۦ كَيۡدٗا فَجَعَلۡنَٰهُمُ ٱلۡأَخۡسَرِينَ
transliteration: Waaradoo bihi kaydan fajaAAalnahumu alakhsareena
translation: And they had designed a plot against him, but We made them themselves the doomed ones. Manzil IV: 21: Anbiya

arabic: وَنَجَّيۡنَٰهُ وَلُوطًا إِلَى ٱلۡأَرۡضِ ٱلَّتِي بَٰرَكۡنَا فِيهَا لِلۡعَٰلَمِينَ
transliteration: Wanajjaynahu walootan ila alardi allatee barakna feeha lilAAalameena
translation: And We saved him and Lot and led them to the land in which We had placed Our blessings for all.
notes: The other Verses (7:137, 17:1, 21:81, 34:18) mentioning the land being divinely blessed do indicate that it is Palestine and the places around, once ruled over by King Solomon. This area had been the bone of contention for Mulims and the Christians during the Crusades earlier. And now it is the bone of contention between the Muslims and the Jews. The Qur’aan tells us that it is a blessed land. The blessings were to be seen only during Solomon’s rein. The land is still potentially blessed; but the blessings are frittered away by the intransigent and warring people, who fail to abide by divine law.

arabic: وَوَهَبۡنَا لَهُۥٓ إِسۡحَٰقَ وَيَعۡقُوبَ نَافِلَةٗۖ وَكُلّٗا جَعَلۡنَا صَٰلِحِينَ
transliteration: Wawahabna lahu ishaqa wayaAAqooba nafilatan wakullan jaAAalna saliheena
translation: And We gave him Isaac – and, in addition, Jacob – and We made them righteous.

arabic: وَجَعَلۡنَٰهُمۡ أَئِمَّةٗ يَهۡدُونَ بِأَمۡرِنَا وَأَوۡحَيۡنَآ إِلَيۡهِمۡ فِعۡلَ ٱلۡخَيۡرَٰتِ وَإِقَامَ ٱلصَّلَوٰةِ وَإِيتَآءَ ٱلزَّكَوٰةِۖ وَكَانُواْ لَنَا عَٰبِدِينَ
transliteration: WajaAAalnahum aimmatan yahdoona biamrina waawhayna ilayhim fiAAla alkhayrati waiqama alssalati waeetaa alzzakati wakanoo lana AAabideena
translation: And We made them leaders who guided people by Our command. And We revealed to them how good deeds are done, how prayers are performed, and how charities are given. And Us certainly they obeyed.

arabic: وَلُوطًا ءَاتَيۡنَٰهُ حُكۡمٗا وَعِلۡمٗا وَنَجَّيۡنَٰهُ مِنَ ٱلۡقَرۡيَةِ ٱلَّتِي كَانَت تَّعۡمَلُ ٱلۡخَبَـٰٓئِثَۚ إِنَّهُمۡ كَانُواْ قَوۡمَ سَوۡءٖ فَٰسِقِينَ
transliteration: Walootan ataynahu hukman waAAilman wanajjaynahu mina alqaryati allatee kanat taAAmalu alkhabaitha innahum kanoo qawma sawin fasiqeena
translation: And Lot – We gave him wisdom and knowledge. And We delivered him from the town that did bad things. They were indeed a wicked, transgressing people (indulging in the sin of homosexuality).
notes: The Qur’aan unequivocally labels homosexuality a sin. But the modern man disagrees! He thinks he is wiser than his Creator.

arabic: وَأَدۡخَلۡنَٰهُ فِي رَحۡمَتِنَآۖ إِنَّهُۥ مِنَ ٱلصَّـٰلِحِينَ
transliteration: Waadkhalnahu fee rahmatina innahu mina alssaliheena
translation: And We took him (Lot) under Our mercy. He was indeed one of the righteous men.

arabic: وَنُوحًا إِذۡ نَادَىٰ مِن قَبۡلُ فَٱسۡتَجَبۡنَا لَهُۥ فَنَجَّيۡنَٰهُ وَأَهۡلَهُۥ مِنَ ٱلۡكَرۡبِ ٱلۡعَظِيمِ
transliteration: Wanoohan ith nada min qablu faistajabna lahu fanajjaynahu waahlahu mina alkarbi alAAatheemi
translation: And Noah – when he called out in an earlier period, We answered him, and delivered him and his family from the acute suffering (of drowning).

arabic: وَنَصَرۡنَٰهُ مِنَ ٱلۡقَوۡمِ ٱلَّذِينَ كَذَّبُواْ بِـَٔايَٰتِنَآۚ إِنَّهُمۡ كَانُواْ قَوۡمَ سَوۡءٖ فَأَغۡرَقۡنَٰهُمۡ أَجۡمَعِينَ
transliteration: Wanasarnahu mina alqawmi allatheena kaththaboo biayatina innahum kanoo qawma sawin faaghraqnahum ajmaAAeena
translation: And We helped him against the people who rejected Our Verses/signs. They were indeed a wicked people, and so We drowned them all.

arabic: وَدَاوُۥدَ وَسُلَيۡمَٰنَ إِذۡ يَحۡكُمَانِ فِي ٱلۡحَرۡثِ إِذۡ نَفَشَتۡ فِيهِ غَنَمُ ٱلۡقَوۡمِ وَكُنَّا لِحُكۡمِهِمۡ شَٰهِدِينَ
transliteration: Wadawooda wasulaymana ith yahkumani fee alharthi ith nafashat feehi ghanamu alqawmi wakunna lihukmihim shahideena
translation: And David and Solomon – when they gave their judgments in the matter of the field wherein the sheep of some people pastured by night. And We bore witness to their judgments.
notes: Solomon was King David’s son. Although the details of the case in question are not given, what we can surmise from the context given is that there was an unauthorized pasturing, on a certain person’s field, by the sheep of others. The matter was brought before King David for decision. The King gave his decision, but son Solomon, who was present at the hearing, suggested a different decision. From the beginning part of the next Verse 79, it is clear, that Solomon’s suggestion was better, and it was ultimately accepted by the King. This episode is narrated here to highlight the sagacity and magnanimity of King David in recognizing and accepting a better decision, though it was at variance with his own and was given by a mere boy. In the ultimate analysis, it is Allah Who puts the right decision in the mind of whomsoever He pleases. Any person concerned should have the large-heartedness to recognize and accept the right decision even when it comes from someone else. It is this attitude that promotes welfare and harmony in public relations.

arabic: فَفَهَّمۡنَٰهَا سُلَيۡمَٰنَۚ وَكُلًّا ءَاتَيۡنَا حُكۡمٗا وَعِلۡمٗاۚ وَسَخَّرۡنَا مَعَ دَاوُۥدَ ٱلۡجِبَالَ يُسَبِّحۡنَ وَٱلطَّيۡرَۚ وَكُنَّا فَٰعِلِينَ
transliteration: Fafahhamnaha sulaymana wakullan atayna hukman waAAilman wasakhkharna maAAa dawooda aljibala yusabbihna waalttayra wakunna faAAileena
translation: And We made Solomon to understand the problem better; but We gave wisdom and knowledge to each one of them. And We made the mountains, and the birds to sing Our praise with David (in the Psalms ). And We did all that.
notes: I.e. Zaboor inspired to David (see Verse 4:163).

arabic: وَعَلَّمۡنَٰهُ صَنۡعَةَ لَبُوسٖ لَّكُمۡ لِتُحۡصِنَكُم مِّنۢ بَأۡسِكُمۡۖ فَهَلۡ أَنتُمۡ شَٰكِرُونَ
transliteration: WaAAallamnahu sanAAata laboosin lakum lituhsinakum min basikum fahal antum shakiroona
translation: And We taught him (David) how to make for you armours that fortify you in your battles. Are you then grateful?
notes: Every innovation – like the armours inspired to David in the ancient past – that has facilitated the ever-increasing ease and convenience in any field of human activity is inspired by Allah Almighty, the real Innovator of all things. HE had inspired David. HE had inspired several Muslims to invent many things that heralded the renaissance of the modern age. And since the Muslims ungratefully consigned the Qur’aan for just ritual reading for occasions like death anniversaries etc., he inspired non-Muslims to make the innovations. But all the innovations are in fact His! But man, in his undeserved arrogance, thinks that it is he who has done it all by dint of his brilliant intellect. He forgets that there was a time when he was nothing. He forgets that his life started as an infinitesimal speck of matter. He forgets that it was not his brilliant brain that fashioned him (including his brain to which he gives credit for all the innovations). He is utterly and shamelessly ungrateful to his Unseen Maker.

arabic: وَلِسُلَيۡمَٰنَ ٱلرِّيحَ عَاصِفَةٗ تَجۡرِي بِأَمۡرِهِۦٓ إِلَى ٱلۡأَرۡضِ ٱلَّتِي بَٰرَكۡنَا فِيهَاۚ وَكُنَّا بِكُلِّ شَيۡءٍ عَٰلِمِينَ
transliteration: Walisulaymana alrreeha AAasifatan tajree biamrihi ila alardi allatee barakna feeha wakunna bikulli shayin AAalimeena
translation: And to Solomon We made subservient the stormy wind, which blew at his command to the land We had blessed. And We did know all things.

arabic: وَمِنَ ٱلشَّيَٰطِينِ مَن يَغُوصُونَ لَهُۥ وَيَعۡمَلُونَ عَمَلٗا دُونَ ذَٰلِكَۖ وَكُنَّا لَهُمۡ حَٰفِظِينَ
transliteration: Wamina alshshayateeni man yaghoosoona lahu wayaAAmaloona AAamalan doona thalika wakunna lahum hafitheena
translation: And of the devils there were those who dived for him and did other work besides. And We were guardians over them.

arabic: ۞وَأَيُّوبَ إِذۡ نَادَىٰ رَبَّهُۥٓ أَنِّي مَسَّنِيَ ٱلضُّرُّ وَأَنتَ أَرۡحَمُ ٱلرَّـٰحِمِينَ
transliteration: Waayyooba ith nada rabbahu annee massaniya alddurru waanta arhamu alrrahimeena
translation: And Job – as he cried to his Lord, “A calamity indeed it is that has afflicted me! And You are the most Merciful.”
notes: Job (Ayyub) was one of the numerous Prophets of Allah. Not much has been stated about him in the Qur’aan except for what we find in these 2 Verses (83 & 84) here and in Verses 38:41 to 38:44. And from the information given, we see that Job was the very epitome of the virtue of patience. He had suffered much – in wealth, health and family – perhaps more than what any other person has ever suffered. But he never lost his faith in Allah. And in the end, he got back what he had lost. The Lord was just preparing him to be set up as patience exemplified.

arabic: فَٱسۡتَجَبۡنَا لَهُۥ فَكَشَفۡنَا مَا بِهِۦ مِن ضُرّٖۖ وَءَاتَيۡنَٰهُ أَهۡلَهُۥ وَمِثۡلَهُم مَّعَهُمۡ رَحۡمَةٗ مِّنۡ عِندِنَا وَذِكۡرَىٰ لِلۡعَٰبِدِينَ
transliteration: Faistajabna lahu fakashafna ma bihi min durrin waataynahu ahlahu wamithlahum maAAahum rahmatan min AAindina wathikra lilAAabideena
translation: And We responded to him and removed whatever affliction he had. And We gave him his family, and as many more with them, out of mercy from Us and for Allah’s worshippers to remember. 21:85 And Ishmael and Idris and Zulkifl – they were all of those who were patient.
notes:
Verses 29:56 and 19:57 also refer to this Prophet.

Verse 38:48 has the only other reference to this Prophet.

arabic: وَإِسۡمَٰعِيلَ وَإِدۡرِيسَ وَذَا ٱلۡكِفۡلِۖ كُلّٞ مِّنَ ٱلصَّـٰبِرِينَ
transliteration: WaismaAAeela waidreesa watha alkifli kullun mina alssabireena
translation: And Ishmael and Idris and Zulkifl – they were all of those who were patient.

arabic: وَأَدۡخَلۡنَٰهُمۡ فِي رَحۡمَتِنَآۖ إِنَّهُم مِّنَ ٱلصَّـٰلِحِينَ
transliteration: Waadkhalnahum fee rahmatina innahum mina alssaliheena
translation: And We admitted them to Our Mercy. They were indeed among the righteous people.

arabic: وَذَا ٱلنُّونِ إِذ ذَّهَبَ مُغَٰضِبٗا فَظَنَّ أَن لَّن نَّقۡدِرَ عَلَيۡهِ فَنَادَىٰ فِي ٱلظُّلُمَٰتِ أَن لَّآ إِلَٰهَ إِلَّآ أَنتَ سُبۡحَٰنَكَ إِنِّي كُنتُ مِنَ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ
transliteration: Watha alnnooni ith thahaba mughadiban fathanna an lan naqdira AAalayhi fanada fee alththulumati an la ilaha illa anta subhanaka innee kuntu mina alththalimeena
translation: And the man of the fish – when he went away in anger , thinking We would not punish him! Then, among the darknesses (of the fish belly), he cried, “There is no god but You, glorified You are! I have indeed been of those who do wrong.”
notes:
Verses 37:139 to 37:148 inform us that it was Prophet Jonas (Yunus) who had been swallowed by a big fish. So we can infer here that the epithet ‘man of the fish’ refers to him.

We learn, from information given in other parts of the Qur’aan, that Jonas had abandoned his people, before Allah permitted him, because they would not believe in him.

arabic: فَٱسۡتَجَبۡنَا لَهُۥ وَنَجَّيۡنَٰهُ مِنَ ٱلۡغَمِّۚ وَكَذَٰلِكَ نُـۨجِي ٱلۡمُؤۡمِنِينَ
transliteration: Faistajabna lahu wanajjaynahu mina alghammi wakathalika nunjee almumineena
translation: Then We responded to him and delivered him from the grief. And thus, do We deliver those who believe.

arabic: وَزَكَرِيَّآ إِذۡ نَادَىٰ رَبَّهُۥ رَبِّ لَا تَذَرۡنِي فَرۡدٗا وَأَنتَ خَيۡرُ ٱلۡوَٰرِثِينَ
transliteration: Wazakariyya ith nada rabbahu rabbi la tatharnee fardan waanta khayru alwaritheena
translation: And Zachariah – when he cried to his Lord, “O my Lord! Leave me not alone (without an heir). And You are the best of inheritors.”
notes: See Verses 19:2 to 19:15 for further details.

arabic: فَٱسۡتَجَبۡنَا لَهُۥ وَوَهَبۡنَا لَهُۥ يَحۡيَىٰ وَأَصۡلَحۡنَا لَهُۥ زَوۡجَهُۥٓۚ إِنَّهُمۡ كَانُواْ يُسَٰرِعُونَ فِي ٱلۡخَيۡرَٰتِ وَيَدۡعُونَنَا رَغَبٗا وَرَهَبٗاۖ وَكَانُواْ لَنَا خَٰشِعِينَ
transliteration: Faistajabna lahu wawahabna lahu yahya waaslahna lahu zawjahu innahum kanoo yusariAAoona fee alkhayrati wayadAAoonana raghaban warahaban wakanoo lana khashiAAeena
translation: And We responded to him and gave him John (Yahya) and made his wife fit to bear the child. They did indeed try to surpass one another in deeds of goodness and they prayed to Us in hope and fear. And they were humble before Us.

arabic: وَٱلَّتِيٓ أَحۡصَنَتۡ فَرۡجَهَا فَنَفَخۡنَا فِيهَا مِن رُّوحِنَا وَجَعَلۡنَٰهَا وَٱبۡنَهَآ ءَايَةٗ لِّلۡعَٰلَمِينَ
transliteration: Waallatee ahsanat farjaha fanafakhna feeha min roohina wajaAAalnaha waibnaha ayatan lilAAalameena
translation: And the lady (Mary) – who guarded her chastity, and We breathed into her of Our spirit and We made her and her son a sign for the worlds.
notes:
The Qur’aan uses this expression for activation of every human life right from Adam (Verses 32.7 to 32.9). With our rd advanced knowledge of embryonic growth, we may now say that this activation takes place, at about the end of the 3 week of conception, when the rudimentary heart of the tiny speck of life inside a womb starts beating. So this Qur’aanic expression should not be misconstrued to substantiate the Christians’ preposterous claim that Jesus was son of God.

Mary and her son Jesus constituted a sign that Allah Almighty can do anything. HE created Adam without His now normal tools of a man and a woman. HE created Eve without the tool of a woman. And He created Jesus without the tool of a man.

arabic: إِنَّ هَٰذِهِۦٓ أُمَّتُكُمۡ أُمَّةٗ وَٰحِدَةٗ وَأَنَا۠ رَبُّكُمۡ فَٱعۡبُدُونِ
transliteration: Inna hathihi ommatukum ommatan wahidatan waana rabbukum faoAAbudooni
translation: Indeed this community of yours is one community, and I am your Lord! So, worship Me.
notes:
I.e. the community that submits to Allah Almighty (refer Verse 2:128 [Manzil I]). Its members may be living in any part of the world, but they all belong to the same single community as declared here by the Creator Himself.

The Muslims are indeed divided now among themselves over their common ground of worshipping the Creator. Satan has succeeded in duping them with the idea that they need the intercession of the Prophet and the saints to take them to Jannah . The satanic deception shall be made clear to them on the Day of Judgment in the Hereafter.

arabic: وَتَقَطَّعُوٓاْ أَمۡرَهُم بَيۡنَهُمۡۖ كُلٌّ إِلَيۡنَا رَٰجِعُونَ
transliteration: WataqattaAAoo amrahum baynahum kullun ilayna rajiAAoona
translation: And they tore apart their (common) duty (of worshipping the sole Creator) among themselves. All shall return to Us.

arabic: فَمَن يَعۡمَلۡ مِنَ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَٰتِ وَهُوَ مُؤۡمِنٞ فَلَا كُفۡرَانَ لِسَعۡيِهِۦ وَإِنَّا لَهُۥ كَٰتِبُونَ
transliteration: Faman yaAAmal mina alssalihati wahuwa muminun fala kufrana lisaAAyihi wainna lahu katiboona
translation: Then one who performs some good deeds and is a believer too – there shall be no concealment of his/her efforts! And surely indeed We will record it (the good deeds).

arabic: وَحَرَٰمٌ عَلَىٰ قَرۡيَةٍ أَهۡلَكۡنَٰهَآ أَنَّهُمۡ لَا يَرۡجِعُونَ
transliteration: Waharamun AAala qaryatin ahlaknaha annahum la yarjiAAoona
translation: And an injunction against any village/town that We have destroyed: they shall not return!
notes: The inhabitants of the village/town destroyed for their transgressions shall not return to this world again to be tested. Their fate is sealed. They will rise again only in the other world to suffer there for ever in Hell-fire. See connecting Verses 96 and 97 below.

arabic: حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا فُتِحَتۡ يَأۡجُوجُ وَمَأۡجُوجُ وَهُم مِّن كُلِّ حَدَبٖ يَنسِلُونَ
transliteration: Hatta itha futihat yajooju wamajooju wahum min kulli hadabin yansiloona
translation: Until the time Gog and Magog are let loose and they descend from every prominent position.
notes: This Biblical expression is used generally as the English equivalent of the Arabic yajooj wa majooj . As per the Bible (Book of Revelation), they are nations under the rule of Satan. In the Qur’aan, the Arabic expression is used in Verse 18:94 as denoting people causing corruption on earth. Applying the same meaning to this Verse (96) here, such corrupt people will get positions of prominence everywhere, and that would be the sign of the approach of the Resurrection Day.

arabic: وَٱقۡتَرَبَ ٱلۡوَعۡدُ ٱلۡحَقُّ فَإِذَا هِيَ شَٰخِصَةٌ أَبۡصَٰرُ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُواْ يَٰوَيۡلَنَا قَدۡ كُنَّا فِي غَفۡلَةٖ مِّنۡ هَٰذَا بَلۡ كُنَّا ظَٰلِمِينَ
transliteration: Waiqtaraba alwaAAdu alhaqqu faitha hiya shakhisatun absaru allatheena kafaroo ya waylana qad kunna fee ghaflatin min hatha bal kunna thalimeena
translation: And the true promise (of Resurrection) shall draw nigh. Then lo! The eyes of those who suppressed the Truth shall open wide in fear. They shall say, “O woe to us! We certainly were heedless of this; nay, we were wicked.”

arabic: إِنَّكُمۡ وَمَا تَعۡبُدُونَ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ حَصَبُ جَهَنَّمَ أَنتُمۡ لَهَا وَٰرِدُونَ
transliteration: Innakum wama taAAbudoona min dooni Allahi hasabu jahannama antum laha waridoona
translation: You and what you worship besides Allah are indeed the fuel of Hell-fire; to it you shall come.

arabic: لَوۡ كَانَ هَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ ءَالِهَةٗ مَّا وَرَدُوهَاۖ وَكُلّٞ فِيهَا خَٰلِدُونَ
transliteration: Law kana haolai alihatan ma waradooha wakullun feeha khalidoona
translation: Had these been gods, they would not have come to it (Hell) and all shall be there eternally.

arabic: لَهُمۡ فِيهَا زَفِيرٞ وَهُمۡ فِيهَا لَا يَسۡمَعُونَ
transliteration: Lahum feeha zafeerun wahum feeha la yasmaAAoona
translation: For them therein wailing and they therein shall not hear.
notes: Those in Hell shall be deaf because they were deaf in this world to Qur’aanic admonition.

arabic: إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ سَبَقَتۡ لَهُم مِّنَّا ٱلۡحُسۡنَىٰٓ أُوْلَـٰٓئِكَ عَنۡهَا مُبۡعَدُونَ
transliteration: Inna allatheena sabaqat lahum minna alhusna olaika AAanha mubAAadoona
translation: Indeed those for whom the good has already gone forth from Us shall be kept far off from it (Hell).

arabic: لَا يَسۡمَعُونَ حَسِيسَهَاۖ وَهُمۡ فِي مَا ٱشۡتَهَتۡ أَنفُسُهُمۡ خَٰلِدُونَ
transliteration: La yasmaAAoona haseesaha wahum fee ma ishtahat anfusuhum khalidoona
translation: They will not hear its (Hell’s) faintest sound, and they shall for ever be in what they desire.

arabic: لَا يَحۡزُنُهُمُ ٱلۡفَزَعُ ٱلۡأَكۡبَرُ وَتَتَلَقَّىٰهُمُ ٱلۡمَلَـٰٓئِكَةُ هَٰذَا يَوۡمُكُمُ ٱلَّذِي كُنتُمۡ تُوعَدُونَ
transliteration: La yahzunuhumu alfazaAAu alakbaru watatalaqqahumu almalaikatu hatha yawmukumu allathee kuntum tooAAadoona
translation: The great fearful event shall not grieve them, and the angels shall meet and tell them, “This is your promised Day!” 21:104 The Day We roll up heaven like the scroll of records is rolled up! As did We originate the first creation, so shall We revive it! A promise binding on Us. We will indeed fulfill it.

arabic: يَوۡمَ نَطۡوِي ٱلسَّمَآءَ كَطَيِّ ٱلسِّجِلِّ لِلۡكُتُبِۚ كَمَا بَدَأۡنَآ أَوَّلَ خَلۡقٖ نُّعِيدُهُۥۚ وَعۡدًا عَلَيۡنَآۚ إِنَّا كُنَّا فَٰعِلِينَ
transliteration: Yawma natwee alssamaa katayyi alssijlli lilkutubi kama badana awwala khalqin nuAAeeduhu waAAdan AAalayna inna kunna faAAileena
translation: The Day We roll up heaven like the scroll of records is rolled up! As did We originate the first creation, so shall We revive it! A promise binding on Us. We will indeed fulfill it.

arabic: وَلَقَدۡ كَتَبۡنَا فِي ٱلزَّبُورِ مِنۢ بَعۡدِ ٱلذِّكۡرِ أَنَّ ٱلۡأَرۡضَ يَرِثُهَا عِبَادِيَ ٱلصَّـٰلِحُونَ
transliteration: Walaqad katabna fee alzzaboori min baAAdi alththikri anna alarda yarithuha AAibadiya alssalihoona
translation: And certainly We did write in the Zabur , after the recounting of events, that My righteous worshippers shall inherit the earth.
notes: The Book (Psalms) revealed to David ( Verse 4:163 [Manzil I]). “The just inherit shall the land, and ever in it dwell” Psalm No. 37.29. [Source: ]

arabic: إِنَّ فِي هَٰذَا لَبَلَٰغٗا لِّقَوۡمٍ عَٰبِدِينَ
transliteration: Inna fee hatha labalaghan liqawmin AAabideena
translation: Indeed in this is a message for people who worship.

arabic: وَمَآ أَرۡسَلۡنَٰكَ إِلَّا رَحۡمَةٗ لِّلۡعَٰلَمِينَ
transliteration: Wama arsalnaka illa rahmatan lilAAalameena
translation: And We have not sent you but as a Mercy to the worlds.
notes: The 2 nd person singular pronoun used here is obviously for the Prophet (peace on him). Now, why has he been described as a mercy for the worlds? As for the interpretation of ‘worlds’ ( aalameen ), please see study note 5 under Verse 1:2. We may therefore infer that the Prophet was sent as a mercy for every individual human life. In what sense was he a mercy? He was Allah’s mercy in the sense that he conveyed to us the Creator’s Own Manual (the Qur’aan) for right conduct of human life. Without this Manual, every man/woman would be just groping in the dark, not knowing what path to follow in this labyrinthine world. And how does one know that the Qur’aanic path is the right path? The distinguishing feature is: the Qur’aan contains no contradictions, and it is inimitable. It has stood this test for over 1400 years now.

arabic: قُلۡ إِنَّمَا يُوحَىٰٓ إِلَيَّ أَنَّمَآ إِلَٰهُكُمۡ إِلَٰهٞ وَٰحِدٞۖ فَهَلۡ أَنتُم مُّسۡلِمُونَ
transliteration: Qul innama yooha ilayya annama ilahukum ilahun wahidun fahal antum muslimoona
translation: Say, “It is revealed to me that the Entity Whom you all ought to worship is but One and Only [Allah]! Will you then submit?”

arabic: فَإِن تَوَلَّوۡاْ فَقُلۡ ءَاذَنتُكُمۡ عَلَىٰ سَوَآءٖۖ وَإِنۡ أَدۡرِيٓ أَقَرِيبٌ أَم بَعِيدٞ مَّا تُوعَدُونَ
transliteration: Fain tawallaw faqul athantukum AAala sawain wain adree aqareebun am baAAeedun ma tooAAadoona
translation: But if they turn away, say, “I have given you a warning in fairness and I know not what you are warned of is near or far.”

arabic: إِنَّهُۥ يَعۡلَمُ ٱلۡجَهۡرَ مِنَ ٱلۡقَوۡلِ وَيَعۡلَمُ مَا تَكۡتُمُونَ
transliteration: Innahu yaAAlamu aljahra mina alqawli wayaAAlamu ma taktumoona
translation: “HE does indeed know what is spoken openly and He knows what you hide.”

arabic: وَإِنۡ أَدۡرِي لَعَلَّهُۥ فِتۡنَةٞ لَّكُمۡ وَمَتَٰعٌ إِلَىٰ حِينٖ
transliteration: Wain adree laAAallahu fitnatun lakum wamataAAun ila heenin
translation: “And I know not but that this (your life in this world) may be a trial for you and a provision to enjoy for a time.”

arabic: قَٰلَ رَبِّ ٱحۡكُم بِٱلۡحَقِّۗ وَرَبُّنَا ٱلرَّحۡمَٰنُ ٱلۡمُسۡتَعَانُ عَلَىٰ مَا تَصِفُونَ
transliteration: Qala rabbi ohkum bialhaqqi warabbuna alrrahmanu almustaAAanu AAala ma tasifoona
translation: He (the Prophet) said, “O my Lord! Decide justly (between me and my recalcitrant people). And our Lord is the Gracious One – the One Whose help is sought against what you (the recalcitrant people) say.”
